class.name: InputHapticEffectCondition
class.brief_description: A haptic effect that represents a conditional force.
class.description: A haptic effect that represents a conditional force. The type can be one of either [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_SPRING], [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_DAMPER], [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_INERTIA], or [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_FRICTION]. Conditions are forces applied in response to current sensor values within the device. In other words, conditions require information about device motion, such as the position or velocity of a joystick handle. In general, conditions are not associated with individual events during a game or other application. They represent ambient phenomena, such as the stiffness or looseness of a flight stick, or the tendency of a steering wheel to return to a straight-ahead position. A condition does not have a predefined magnitude. The magnitude is scaled in proportion to the movement or position of the input object. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

method.name: set_effect_type
method.description: Sets the haptic effect type. The type for a conditional haptic effect can be either [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_SPRING], [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_DAMPER], [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_INERTIA], or [constant InputHapticEffect.HAPTIC_EFFECT_FRICTION].

members.member: The offset from the zero reading of the appropriate sensor value at which the condition begins to be applied. For a spring effect, the neutral point - that is, the point along the axis at which the spring would be considered at rest - would be defined by the offset for the condition. For a damper, the offset would define the greatest velocity value for which damping force is zero. Offset is not normally used for inertia or friction effects. Each of the axis values are between [code]-1.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

The deadband is a zone around the offset of an axis at which the condition is not active. In the case of a spring that is at rest in the middle of an axis, the deadband enlarges this area of rest. Not all devices support deadband. Each of the axis values are between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A multiplier that scales the effect towards the negative side. Each of the axis values are between [code]-1.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

Level when joystick is at the negative side. Each of the axis values are between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A multiplier that scales the effect towards the positive side. Each of the axis values are between [code]-1.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

Level when joystick is at the positive side. Each of the axis values are between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.
